title: Post-operative eye wound review

medication.text: Supports documenting expected visual changes, possible complication symptoms, activity or driving restrictions, contact arrangements, next appointment, vision change, spectacles, second-eye discussion, visual function, quality of life, and postoperative review processes without mapping medicines, doses, or autonomous urgency.
medication.rationale: Supports postoperative cataract review, complications and follow-up context for post-operative eye wound review; no decision is generated.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting expected visual changes, possible complication symptoms, activity or driving restrictions, contact arrangements, next appointment, vision change, spectacles, second-eye discussion, visual function, quality of life, and postoperative review processes without mapping medicines, doses, or autonomous urgency.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.1.6 and 1.9.1–1.9.3 — postoperative information and assessment
exact_section.locator: recommendations 1.1.5–1.1.6 and 1.9.1–1.9.3; official page lines 101–136 and 473–489

follow_up.text: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.
follow_up.rationale: Supports general surgical-site infection recognition context for post-operative eye wound review; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.

source.title: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment — Recommendations

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Procedure and date, eye, wound site, pain, discharge, redness, swelling, vision, sutures, examination, infection assessment and surgeon plan are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for post-operative eye wound review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
